_comment: One file per procedure_code. Distilled from the matching pair in PM JAY/Standard Treatment Guideline/master/<condition>/ : _Medco.pdf (clinical + mandatory docs) + _PPD.CPD.pdf (PPD/CPD processing checklists). Filename = procedure_code. This is what the Documentation Checker, Query Predictor, and Medical Justification agents read AFTER the picker has chosen the code.
clinical_indications: Abscess on Radiology with size < 2.5 cm.
Largest abscess for aspiration when multiple are present.

preauth.label: Clinical notes with signs, symptoms, indications, planned line of management and advice for admission

ppd_preauth.q: Clinical notes including history, symptoms, signs, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment and advice for admission?
Did the patient present with headache/giddiness/convulsion/severe vomiting/ heaviness of head/ fever?
Did CECT/MRI Brain confirm the diagnosis?
Was clinical presentation and imaging indicative of surgery?

cpd_claim.q: Are the detailed ICPs with daily vitals and line of treatment?
Are the detailed procedure / Operative Notes available?
Is the Discharge summary with follow-up advise at the time of discharge?
Was CT brain pre and post-surgery report submitted?

common_queries: Clinical notes are missing or incomplete regarding history, symptoms, signs, vitals, examination findings, planned line of treatment, or advice for admission.
Patient's clinical presentation or imaging was not indicative of brain abscess requiring surgical intervention.
CECT/MRI Brain report does not confirm the diagnosis of brain abscess at pre-authorization.
Mandatory documents for claim submission (ICPs, operative notes, histopathology report, or pre/post-op CT brain) are missing or incomplete.

clinical_key_pointers: Focal intracranial infection initiated as an area of cerebritis and evolves into a collection of pus surrounded by a vascularized capsule.

Pathogenesis: Contiguous spread (Middle ear, Mastoid, PNS), Hematogenous (Lung abscess, Skin Osteomyelitis, Pelvic infections, Cyanotic Heart Disease), Trauma (Dural breach and Foreign body entering into the cranial cavity).
Stages of infection: Early cerebritis (1-3 days), Late cerebritis (4-9 days), Early capsular Formation (10-13 days), Late capsule Formation (14 days and later).
Clinical presentation: Headache, Fever, Focal Neurological Deficits, Altered mental status sensorium, Seizures, Nausea/vomiting, Nuchal rigidity, Papilledema.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY.
